raw: {"score":3,"rationale":"The project is clean enough: backend code is separated into db, policy, routes, services, and errors; policy constants are centralized; HTTP handlers delegate to services; and madge reports no cycles or orphans. It does not reach 4 because services call better-sqlite3 directly, HTTP responses expose service-returned DB rows, the frontend is organized by technical role rather than feature ownership, and there is no evident shared frontend/backend type boundary.","evidence":["backend-tree.txt shows clear backend layers: db/, policy/, routes/, services/, errors.ts, app.ts.","frontend-tree.txt shows a conventional split with api/, components/, pages/, but features/ is empty, so it is not feature-owned architecture.","madge.json reports \"circular\": [] and \"orphans\": [].","policy/constants.ts centralizes rule values including MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS = 5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS = 14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY = 50, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS = 48, and FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS = 500.","loan-service.ts imports policy functions from ../policy/index.js and uses domain errors from ../errors.js, which supports separation of business rules from HTTP handling.","loan-handler.ts validates requests with zod and calls loanService.borrow / loanService.returnLoan instead of embedding SQL in the route handler.","loan-service.ts directly imports better-sqlite3 and calls db.prepare throughout, so services are coupled to raw DB access rather than a thin query abstraction.","loan-service.ts returns LoanRow from SELECT * and loan-handler.ts responds with res.status(201).json(loan), showing DB-shaped rows pass through to HTTP responses.","file-sizes.csv has no god files or production files over 300 lines; the largest production excerpts are frontend/src/pages/MemberDetail.tsx at 219 lines and frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx at 191 lines, while backend/src/services/api.test.ts is 355 lines."],"caps_or_penalties":["No madge cycle cap applies.","No god-file cap applies.","Score capped below 4 by raw DB access in services, role-based frontend organization, and no visible shared type boundary."]}

parsed.rationale: The project is clean enough: backend code is separated into db, policy, routes, services, and errors; policy constants are centralized; HTTP handlers delegate to services; and madge reports no cycles or orphans. It does not reach 4 because services call better-sqlite3 directly, HTTP responses expose service-returned DB rows, the frontend is organized by technical role rather than feature ownership, and there is no evident shared frontend/backend type boundary.
evidence: backend-tree.txt shows clear backend layers: db/, policy/, routes/, services/, errors.ts, app.ts.
frontend-tree.txt shows a conventional split with api/, components/, pages/, but features/ is empty, so it is not feature-owned architecture.

policy/constants.ts centralizes rule values including MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS = 5, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS = 14, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY = 50, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS = 48, and FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS = 500.
loan-service.ts imports policy functions from ../policy/index.js and uses domain errors from ../errors.js, which supports separation of business rules from HTTP handling.
loan-handler.ts validates requests with zod and calls loanService.borrow / loanService.returnLoan instead of embedding SQL in the route handler.
loan-service.ts directly imports better-sqlite3 and calls db.prepare throughout, so services are coupled to raw DB access rather than a thin query abstraction.
loan-service.ts returns LoanRow from SELECT * and loan-handler.ts responds with res.status(201).json(loan), showing DB-shaped rows pass through to HTTP responses.

file-sizes.csv has no god files or production files over 300 lines; the largest production excerpts are frontend/src/pages/MemberDetail.tsx at 219 lines and frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx at 191 lines, while backend/src/services/api.test.ts is 355 lines.
caps_or_penalties: Score capped below 4 by raw DB access in services, role-based frontend organization, and no visible shared type boundary.
